mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds into sustained corporate earnings improvements and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, triggering immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, breaking the negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation simultaneously decelerates, the macro environment shifts toward a soft landing scenario that reduces recession risk and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and supports durable goods consumption, creating a reliable upward drift in S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.10, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms an economic downturn that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the first week following data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%, negating the bearish signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable retail sales during a disinflationary phase indicates resilient manufacturing capacity that supports corporate margins and drives positive monthly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint positive trend in INDPRO and RSAFS during disinflation fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.
